rulings.Case Title: STEVEN STEINHAUER et al VS. EDWARD J. MURPHY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Modification Of Order Confirmiong Sale Of Real Property; Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 30, 2012, Line 1. CROSS DEFENDANT EDWARD MURPHY'S Motion For Modification Of Order Confirming Sale Of Real Property. Granted. The sum of $ 822,343.29 represents Mr. Murphy's proceeds as the seller. The Sales Referee is directed to instruct to direct First American Title to transfer 822,343.29, plus any accrued interest on this amount, to a 1031 exchange company selected by Mr. Murphy. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: FRANK ALIOTO et al VS. WILLIAM F. GARLOCK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For 473 Relief
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 30, 2012, Line 3. PLAINTIFFS FRANK ALIOTO, ROSEMARY ALIOTO, GEORGE BERBERICH, FRANK ANGELO ALIOTO, AS TRUSTEE OF THE ALIOTO, ROSEMARY ALIOTO, AS TRUSTEE'S Motion For 473 Relief. Denied. Plaintiffs have not satisfied their burden of showing that they are entitled to either mandatory or discretionary relief under Code of Civil Procedure section 473(b). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: FRANK ALIOTO et al VS. WILLIAM F. GARLOCK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Tax Costs Claimed By Hamilton Ricci
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 30, 2012, Line 3. PLAINTIFFS FRANK ALIOTO, ROSEMARY ALIOTO, FRANK ANGELO ALIOTO, AS TRUSTEE OF THE ALIOTO'S Motion To Tax Costs Claimed By Hamilton Ricci. Granted as to the request for unpaid discovery sanctions of $ 2,660.00 and denied as to all other claimed costs. An unpaid discovery sanction is not a cost per Code of Civil Procedure section 1033.5. Plainitffs have not shown that any other claimed costs are unrecoverable. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. Defendant's counsel is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: HEZZACK DANIELS VS. RECOLOGY INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Recology Inc. (From Case# 508886), Recology San Francisco (From Case# 508886)'S Motion To Deem Requests For Admission Admitted; Compel Responses To Interrogatories, And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 30, 2012, Line 27. Defendants Recology Inc. (From Case# 508886), Recology San Francisco (From Case# 508886)'S Motion To Deem Requests For Admission Admitted; Compel Responses To Interrogatories, And Request For Sanctions. The court adopts in full the recommendation of Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne as follows: PLAINTIFFS DISPUTE RECEIPT OF THE REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION, BUT HAVE NOW FILED RESPONSES WHICH PRINCIPALLY CONSIST OF DENIALS AND A RESPONSE TO FORM INTERROGATORY 17.1 WHICH PURPORTS TO APPLY TO EACH SEPARATE DENIAL. THESE RESPONSES DO NOT COMPLY WITH THE REQUIREMENTS OF FORM INTERROGATORY 17.1. ACCORDINGLY, PLAINTIFFS ARE EACH ORDERED TO SEPARATELY RESPOND TO EACH REQUEST FOR ADMISSION, WITHOUT OBJECTIONS, AND, TO THE EXTENT THAT THE RESPONSE IS ANYTHING OTHER AN UNQUALIFIED ADMISSION, HE WILL PROVIDE THE DETAILED INFORMATION REQUESTED BY FORM INTERROGATORY AS TO EACH SUCH DENIAL. SUCH RESPONSES SHALL BE SERVED NO LATER THAN OCTOBER 1 2012. PLAINTIFFS AND THEIR COUNSEL WILL PAY TO DEFENDANT RECOLOGY SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $2,500.00 BY OCTOBER 1, 2012. NOTHING IN THIS ORDER SHALL BE COSNTRUED AS PERMITTING ANY PLAINTIFF TO WITHDRAW OR AMEND AN ADMISSION MADE IN RESPONSE TO A REQUEST FOR ADMISSION WITHOUT FIRST COMPLYING WITH CALIFORNIA CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 2033.300(A). The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery motion. If either side desires a court reporter, counsel must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: SALVATORE MINEO INDIVIDUALLY, AND ON BEHALF OF ALL et al VS. CITY SIGHTSEEING CORPORATION, A DELAWARE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Class Certification; Memo Of P&A;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 30, 2012, Line 6. PLAINTIFFS SALVATORE MINEO INDIVIDUALLY, AND ON BEHALF OF ALL, GINA SCHEMBARI INDIVIDUALLY, AND ON BEHALF OF ALL, PHILLIP NAILS, BENJAMIN DAUX, DAVID HAYWARD'S Motion For Class Certification. Granted in part. All three proposed classes are certified only as to claims for unpaid overtime under California law. The motion is denied as to all other claims. Individualized issues predominate over class issues in determining whether defendant issued accurate written itemized statements of income and deductions, compensated plaintiffs for attending meetings (reporting time penalties), issued payroll checks with sufficient funds; and compensated plaintiffs for unrelieved meal and break periods. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. Plaintiffs' counsel is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: HITACHI GLOBAL STORAGE TECHNOLOGIES, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Attorney'S Fees
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 30, 2012, Line 9. DEFENDANT JAMES GHIELMETTI'S Motion For Attorney's Fees. Denied. Civil Code 1717 applies because no judgment was rendered against Mr. Ghilmetti. There is no prevailing party between plaintiff and Mr. Ghielmetti per Hilltop Investment Assoc v. Leon (1994) 28 Cal.App.4th 462. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: MISCHA JACKSON VS. CYNTHIA LIN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Answers To Form Interrogatories, And Demand For Production; Memo Of P & A; Req. For Reasonable Sanctions And Attorneys' Fees; Dec And Pos
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, August 30, 2012, Line 2, DEFENDANT JIAN CHEN'S Motion To Compel Answers To Form Interrogatories, And Demand For Production; Request For Reasonable Sanctions And Attorneys' Fees. Pro Tem Judge Trent Thornley, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JANET HALEY VS. HEIDI ANN RICHARDSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 30, 2012, Line 11. DEFENDANT HEIDI RICHARDSON'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary judgment is denied and the alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to the first, second, and third causes of action and granted as to the fourth cause of action. Plaintiff presented evidence to raise triable issues whether Defendant acted outrageously and intentionally and caused Plaintiff to suffer actual and severe emotional distress, Defendant's statements were false and defamatory on their face, Plaintiff suffered special damages, Plaintiff had an economic relationship with the probability of future economic benefit, and Defendant's actions were wrongful. The fourth cause of action fails because Defendant owed no duty to Plaintiff as a matter of law. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with CCP § 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ALI BAALOUACH VS. CHICAGO TITLE INSURANCE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Ali Baalouach'S Motion For Reconsideration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 30, 2012, Line 21. Plaintiff Ali Baalouach'S Motion For Reconsideration. Denied. Mr. Baalouach has not presented any new law, facts or circumstances warranting reconsideration. The statutory deadlines for an anti-SLAPP motion are not arbitrary. The anti-SLAPP motion does not violate Mr. Baalouach's right to petition. Chicago Title is awarded attorney fees of $1500. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
